Aamir, visibly emotional during the viewing, lauded co-star Sai Pallavi as the "best actress we have in our country today" and praised Junaid's efforts.

The film demonstrated remarkable growth over its sixth weekend, outperforming competition. Despite new releases, its enduring popularity is evident in its sustained box office success.

articles.title: New mom Hailee Steinfeld gives rare glimpse into family life with emotional note about her brother

Griffin discusses launching his business and staying true to his vision, while both highlight the importance of communication in their bond. The feature also adds a lighter touch as Griffin shares his excitement about becoming an uncle, giving readers a more intimate view of Steinfeld’s life beyond public headlines.

articles.title: Aries Horoscope Today, April 27, 2026: Bold Decisions Emerge as Emotional Fog Clears Rapidly
articles.description: Aries, today feels like a breath after intensity. The emotional pressure of the past days begins to dissolve, and you may feel lighter, not because everything is resolved, but because you are no longer carrying the same emotional weight. Let’s find out what astrologer Sandeep Agarwal predicts in this aries horoscope today, where themes of bold decisions and strategic awareness emerge through your aries daily horoscope, alongside deeper clarity reflected in today's aries horoscope, shaping how this aries horoscope today unfolds, while aligning with the evolving rhythm of daily astrology for aries.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Immanuel Kant: 'If you punish a child for being naughty, and reward him for being good, he will do right just for the reward...' - Why morality should not depend on rewards or fear explained by the German philosopher
articles.description: Immanuel Kant’s quote of the day highlights a key concern in moral education: if children are taught to behave well only for rewards and avoid punishment, they may grow up linking morality with personal gain rather than duty. According to Kant, this kind of upbringing can shape individuals who act based on advantage instead of principle, especially when they discover that the real world does not always reward good actions or punish bad ones.

articles.title: Who was ‘Hell Lady’ Kazuko Hosoki, the inspiration for Netflix’s ‘Straight to Hell’? The rise, fame, controversy, and legacy explained
articles.description: Who was ‘Hell Lady’ Kazuko Hosoki, the inspiration for Netflix’s ‘Straight to Hell’? This explainer follows the life of Kazuko Hosoki, from post-war struggle and nightlife business to national fame as a fortune teller, author, and TV personality. It covers her Six Star Astrology system, record book sales, media career, controversies, and lasting cultural influence highlighted again by Netflix series Straight to Hell.

articles.title: How Rain outshone ‘Paperback Writer’ as The Beatles’ psychedelic studio breakthrough
articles.description: The Beatles' song 'Rain' from 1966 is a standout track. It features John Lennon's vocals and Ringo Starr's unique drumming. The song pioneered backward vocals in popular music. It showcases the band's experimental phase. 'Rain' captures a unique mood of peacefulness. The song's hypnotic quality draws listeners in.

While channeling his homeless character from 'Being Flynn', his elaborate disguise was so convincing that the hotel staff mistook him for an actual drifter, barring his entry.

articles.title: When Raghu Rai’s Perfect Timing Made It Look Like The President Pulled Manekshaw’s Moustache
articles.description: Raghu Rai’s work was never about grand setups or extraordinary subjects; it was his impeccable timing that brought out personality, wit, and meaning within a single frame.
